mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs, embeddings, and speech-to-text applications. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models in production environments, offering intelligent scaling and optimized routing. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient hardware resources and the absence of dependencies on other systems like Istio or Knative.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://charts.kubeai.org

helm repo update

```

> Add the official KubeAI Helm repository.
> Update your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/substratusai/kubeai/main/deploy/crds/kubeai_v1alpha1_model_crd.yaml

```

> Install the KubeAI model CRD.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs KubeAI in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --version 0.23.1 --namespace kubeai --create-namespace

```

> Install KubeAI in the 'kubeai' namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
Check the status of the KubeAI pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

kubectl get crd

```

> List all pods in the kubeai namespace.
> Verify that the KubeAI CRDs are installed.

steps.title: Create an example Model Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example model resource to test the KubeAI deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  modelType: 'llm'
  modelName: 'gpt-3'
EOF

```

> Create a sample model resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  modelType: 'llm'
  modelName: 'gpt-3'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Model Custom Resource
steps.description:
Check the status of the created model resource to ensure it is ready.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai

```

> List all model resources in the kubeai namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all model resources and configurations.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> Uninstall KubeAI from the kubeai namespace.
> Delete the kubeai namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your model configurations. This step will upgrade KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --version 0.23.1 --namespace kubeai

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Backup current model configurations.
> Upgrade KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the KubeAI pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get models -n kubeai -o yaml > backup-models.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to install CRD'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRD installation may have failed due to network issues or incorrect URLs.

**Fix:**
Retry the CRD installation command or check your network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the model configuration is incorrect or if the required resources are not available.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model resource may not exist or may not be correctly defined.

**Fix:**
Verify the model resource definition and ensure it is applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources (CPU, memory) to schedule the KubeAI pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust the resource requests in the model definition.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running with the status 'Running' and the model resource listed with 'Ready' status when everything is working.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kubeai.substratus.ai/v1alpha1
kind: Model
metadata:
  name: example-model
spec:
  modelType: 'llm'
  modelName: 'gpt-3'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
